description_long: This object consists of three daisy-like flowers with white petals radiating from a central yellow disc, supported by slender green stems. The petals are broad, slightly overlapping, and arranged in a single whorl around each flower's center. The stems are thin and upright, emerging from a cluster of elongated, lanceolate green leaves at the base. The leaves are narrow, pointed, and have a slightly wavy texture, adding to the naturalistic appearance. The flowers are positioned at varying heights, creating a visually dynamic cluster typical of wildflowers or ornamental plantings. The overall form is delicate and organic, suitable for placement in parks, gardens, or landscaped urban areas.
description: A cluster of three daisy-like flowers with white petals and yellow centers, supported by slender green stems and lanceolate leaves. The arrangement is naturalistic and visually dynamic, ideal for urban landscaping.
description_view_0: Front view showing the largest flower head centered, with two additional flowers visible behind and above, and a clear display of the green leaves at the base.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the profile of the flowers, with the petals' thickness and the upward angle of the stems more apparent.
description_view_2: Rear view showing the backs of the flower heads and the arrangement of the stems and leaves, with the yellow centers less prominent.
description_view_3: Opposite side view emphasizing the curvature of the stems and the layered arrangement of the leaves and petals.
attribute_petal_arrangement: The petals are arranged in a radial, single-whorled configuration around the central disc, with slight overlapping and clear radial symmetry typical of Asteraceae.
